moderate: internal 8x/9x number slide — out of scope per EXP-161 notes; the current prose already splits cleanly between '8.2× at the 70°S coast' and '9× at the full rim', so the slide is acceptable but could be tightened further if the decider revisits.
find_target: The simplest geometric test — rim circumnavigation. The dome's disc has a radius of 20,015 km (the ice wall). On a flat disc, the circumference at radial distance r is simply 2πr. Antarctica, which forms the dome's rim, has a circumference of 2π × 20,015 ≈ 125,760 km. Even well inside the rim — at a radius corresponding to the Antarctic coast (~70°S on the globe, roughly r ≈ 17,800 km on the dome) — the circumference would be ~112,000 km. The measured distance to circumnavigate Antarctica at ~70°S is approximately 13,700 km — confirmed by satellite-tracked circumnavigation records (Jules Verne Trophy and Vendée Globe routes log distance by elapsed time, speed, fuel consumption, and continuous GPS) and by Antarctic research-vessel deployments. This is a factor-of-eight discrepancy at the 70°S coast (dome predicts ~112,000 km vs. measured ~13,700 km, ratio ≈ 8.2×); the full rim figure (125,760 km) gives a factor of nine. No coordinate formula, aetheric refraction, or refractive index n(r) can resolve it: refraction bends light, but ships move through water. A vessel's hull displacement, fuel burn, and elapsed time are physical measurements immune to optical effects. The dome's own evolution log acknowledges the fundamental geometric problem. Version 22 concluded: 'Southern arc distances 2x too long — FAILS. Globe wins cross-equator.' The V13 Finsler system was introduced specifically to address this, but even V13 does not publish a formula for n(r) that could be independently tested against circumnavigation data. The rim circumference test predates and supersedes all coordinate formula debates: if Antarctica is a rim, sailing around it should take eight to nine times longer than it does.
replacement_html: The simplest geometric test — rim circumnavigation. The dome's disc has a radius of 20,015 km (the ice wall). On a flat disc, the circumference at radial distance r is simply 2πr. Antarctica, which forms the dome's rim, has a circumference of 2π × 20,015 ≈ 125,760 km. Even well inside the rim — at a radius corresponding to the Antarctic coast — the circumference stays enormous. Under the standard flat-earth azimuthal-equidistant mapping, where latitude φ projects linearly to disc radius via r = 20,015 × (90° − φ)/180°, the 70°S coast sits at r = 20,015 × 160/180 ≈ 17,791 km, giving a circumference of 2π × 17,791 ≈ 111,800 km. The measured distance to circumnavigate Antarctica at ~70°S is approximately 13,700 km (the globe value 2π × 6,371 × cos 70° = 13,691 km), confirmed by satellite-tracked circumnavigation records (distance logged by elapsed time, speed, fuel consumption, and continuous GPS) and by satellite altimetry of the Antarctic coastline. This is a factor-of-eight discrepancy at the 70°S coast (dome predicts ~111,800 km vs. measured ~13,700 km, ratio ≈ 8.2×); the full rim figure (125,760 km) gives a factor of nine. No coordinate formula, aetheric refraction, or refractive index n(r) can resolve it: refraction bends light, but ships move through water. A vessel's hull displacement, fuel burn, and elapsed time are physical measurements immune to optical effects. A sophisticated defender might instead argue that the Finsler metric compresses physical space itself near the rim — that a ship logging 13,700 km of fuel-burn distance is covering 125,760 km of "dome-Euclidean" distance. But a flat-disc metric that compresses distances by exactly the factor needed to match a sphere is a sphere in disguise — at that point the dome has lost its geometric distinctiveness and become a renamed globe projection. Nor does the projection choice rescue the argument: the V12/V13 system replaces the linear AE mapping with H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) km and elliptic-integral arc lengths, but any monotonic latitude→radius mapping that places Antarctica on or near the rim of a 20,015 km disc still yields a rim circumference of ~125,760 km and a coastal circumference of the same order. The disc radius, not the projection, sets the scale. The dome's own evolution log shows the author has conceded a related geometric failure in the past — but a smaller one. Version 22 concluded, in the specific context of cross-equatorial city-pair distances computed under V9/V11's circular-disc + WGS84 coordinate formula: "Southern arc distances 2x too long — FAILS. Globe wins cross-equator." V12/V13 claim to have narrowed that 2× coordinate-formula error to ~5–10% using H(r), elliptic-integral arc lengths, and a two-zone topology. The rim circumnavigation problem is a separate and larger failure: a factor of 8–9, not 2×, and it follows from the disc radius itself rather than from any coordinate formula. It predates and supersedes all coordinate-formula debates: if Antarctica is a rim, sailing around it should take eight to nine times longer than it does.

v22_quote_analysis.exact_quote: Southern arc distances 2x too long — FAILS. Globe wins cross-equator.
v22_quote_analysis.surrounding_context: Evolution log entry under '=== v22 ADDRESSED DIRECTLY ===' header. Next line: 'This was correct for circular disc + WGS84 coordinates.' Followed by a list of what changed: WIN-027 (quadratic law), WIN-030 (ellipse), V12 H(r), Oslo-Stockholm 0.0% error.
v22_quote_analysis.what_v22_actually_conceded: A 2× coordinate-formula error on cross-equatorial city pairs (e.g., SYD-EZE, JNB-AKL) under the V9/V11-era assumption of a circular disc with WGS84-style coordinates. The admission is explicitly scoped to that regime: 'correct for circular disc + WGS84 coordinates.'
v22_quote_analysis.what_v22_did_not_concede: The 8-9x rim circumnavigation problem. That failure mode does not appear in the evolution log under any version — V12/V13 claim to have solved the coordinate-formula side (down to ~5-10%) but have never addressed the disc-radius-sets-the-scale problem.
v22_quote_analysis.prior_framing_weakness: The EXP-126 prose framed the quote as 'The dome's own evolution log acknowledges the fundamental geometric problem' immediately after discussing the 8-9x rim discrepancy. A careful dome defender would correctly point out that v22 admitted a different, smaller problem.
v22_quote_analysis.corrected_framing: The new prose explicitly scopes what v22 conceded (2× coordinate-formula on cross-equatorial city pairs under circular disc + WGS84) and distinguishes it from the rim problem (8-9×, follows from disc radius, independent of coordinate formula). This turns a potential counter-attack vector into a secondary data point: the author has acknowledged geometric failures before, but not this one.

ae_projection_derivation.why_this_projection: The standard flat-earth azimuthal-equidistant mapping — used in the UN-flag-style flat-earth diagram and assumed in most dome-community calculations. North pole at r = 0, equator at r = R/2 = 10,007.5 km, south pole (rim) at r = R = 20,015 km. Latitude maps linearly to radius.
ae_projection_derivation.projection_independence_note: The new prose explicitly states that the rim circumference result is insensitive to projection choice. The V12/V13 non-linear H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) exponential and elliptic-integral arc lengths remap radius in altitude and in distance integrals, but they cannot change the flat-disc circumference formula 2πr — that is a topological consequence of flat discs, not a property of any particular projection. The disc radius sets the scale; the projection only shuffles latitudes around inside that scale.

kernel_of_truth: The v22 quote IS a real and remarkable concession — it demonstrates the dome author has engaged with geometric failures of his own system and publicly acknowledged them. That intellectual honesty is genuine and deserves acknowledgment. The mathematical evolution from V9 (circular disc, WGS84) to V12 (elliptical disc, H(r) firmament) to V13 (two-zone topology, Finsler metric, elliptic integrals) shows real mathematical sophistication. The revised prose keeps the quote and credits that evolution ('V12/V13 claim to have narrowed that 2× coordinate-formula error to ~5–10% using H(r), elliptic-integral arc lengths, and a two-zone topology') rather than dismissing it. What the kernel doesn't save: the rim problem is not a coordinate-formula problem. It is a disc-radius problem. No amount of metric tuning can make a flat disc with radius 20,015 km produce an Antarctic coastal circumference of 13,700 km without redefining the metric itself to match a sphere — which collapses the model into a renamed globe.

anticipated_objections.objection: You're still misusing the v22 quote. He conceded 2× on city pairs, not 8× on the rim — why mention it at all?
anticipated_objections.response: The new prose explicitly scopes what v22 conceded (2× coordinate-formula on cross-equatorial city pairs under circular disc + WGS84) and distinguishes it from the rim problem. The quote appears as evidence that the author has a track record of acknowledging geometric failures — not as evidence that he has acknowledged THIS failure. The framing now says 'a related geometric failure ... but a smaller one' and walks through exactly what was and was not conceded.

anticipated_objections.objection: The AE projection is a strawman — V13 doesn't use linear azimuthal-equidistant mapping.
anticipated_objections.response: The new prose pre-empts this: it walks through the linear AE mapping to derive the 17,791 km figure explicitly, then notes that V12/V13 use H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) and elliptic-integral arc lengths, and then shows why it doesn't matter — any monotonic latitude→radius mapping that places Antarctica on or near the rim of a 20,015 km disc produces a circumference of order 125,760 km. The rim argument is projection-independent because it follows from the disc radius, not from any particular latitude mapping.

anticipated_objections.objection: The Finsler metric handles this — a 13,700 km ship-log translates to 125,760 km of dome-Euclidean distance via the compression factor near the rim.
anticipated_objections.response: The new prose deploys the response in-section: a flat-disc metric that compresses distances by exactly the factor needed to match a sphere IS a sphere in disguise. At that point the Finsler tensor has been tuned to reproduce spherical geodesics, and the dome has lost its geometric distinctiveness. The claim 'flat earth with correction factor X' is mathematically equivalent to 'sphere' when X is tuned to make all measurements match a sphere.

anticipated_objections.objection: Your 13,700 km figure relies on modern yacht races and satellite-tracked ships at 70°S, but those don't exist — yacht races run at 40-55°S and the Soviet Ob didn't circumnavigate at 70°S.
anticipated_objections.response: This is a real weakness in the citations (flagged by curmudgeon as a major hole, hole #1). EXP-161 scope was limited to the v22 quote and AE projection issues; the citation upgrade is deferred to a future EXP. The substance of the 13,700 km figure is independently supported by SCAR Antarctic Digital Database coastline surveys and by the trivial globe calculation 2π × 6,371 × cos 70° = 13,691 km. The prose could be tightened to cite SCAR/IBCSO/ICESat-2 altimetry instead of yacht races, and this is recommended as the next patch.

tldr_note: The TLDR was updated by the decider on 2026-04-14 and already mentions the factor-of-eight discrepancy. Current TLDR is factually accurate and does not require changes for this patch.
decider_notes: This is a minimal surgical patch limited to paragraph 4 of Section 7.1. It (1) scopes the v22 quote correctly, (2) shows the AE projection derivation explicitly, and (3) bonus — pre-empts the Finsler-metric defense that EXP-126 anticipated_objections #2 flagged but which did not make it into the prose. Paragraphs 1-3 and the TLDR are unchanged. Recommend: apply find_target → replacement_html in data/sections.json part7.html. The citation cleanup (Soviet Ob / HMS Endurance / yacht races at 70°S) is NOT addressed in this EXP and should be tracked as a separate future item if desired. Verdict_holds remains true; this is a quality-of-integration patch, not a substance change.

v22_quote_source_check.surrounding_context_verified: Header line 54: "=== v22 ADDRESSED DIRECTLY ==="; line 57: "This was correct for circular disc + WGS84 coordinates."; line 9 (elsewhere in same file): "v22 found circular disc fails on southern distances — that was correct and drove the ellipse discovery." This confirms v22 conceded a coordinate-formula / city-pair failure, NOT the rim circumnavigation problem. Current prose framing ("a related geometric failure in the past — but a smaller one ... V12/V13 claim to have narrowed that 2× coordinate-formula error to ~5–10%") is accurate.
v22_quote_source_check.verdict: Current prose framing of v22 quote is accurate and appropriately scoped. The hole is addressed.

ae_projection_derivation_check.projection_independence_text: any monotonic latitude→radius mapping that places Antarctica on or near the rim of a 20,015 km disc still yields a rim circumference of ~125,760 km and a coastal circumference of the same order. The disc radius, not the projection, sets the scale.
ae_projection_derivation_check.verdict: Derivation and projection-independence are both explicit in the integrated prose. The hole is addressed.

optional_minor_tightening.issue: The prose attributes the pre-ellipse coordinate formula to "V9/V11" specifically ("V9/V11's circular-disc + WGS84 coordinate formula"). The dome's evolution log (line 57) says only "This was correct for circular disc + WGS84 coordinates" without specifying V9 or V11. The V9/V11 labels appear elsewhere in the evolution log for specific coordinate-formula bug fixes under V12 ("V12 ... V9 theta bug fixed"). Attributing v22 to V9/V11 specifically is an inference and not directly supported by the evolution log.
optional_minor_tightening.suggested_replacement: V9/V11's circular-disc + WGS84 coordinate formula → the dome's pre-ellipse circular-disc + WGS84 coordinate formula
optional_minor_tightening.impact_if_unchanged: Very small — a sharp dome defender could point out "that is an inference, not a direct quote from our evolution log," but the substantive point is unchanged. Not blocking. Recommend as future polish, not a blocker.
optional_minor_tightening.replacement: the dome's pre-ellipse circular-disc + WGS84 coordinate formula

analyst_verification.overall_verdict: Both remaining moderate holes (v22 quote scoping + AE projection derivation) are ADDRESSED in the integrated prose. One minor wording polish (V9/V11 → pre-ellipse) is suggested but not required. Tracker can be marked complete.

verification_report.verification_method: Compared current data/sections.json part7 SEC-7.1 paragraph 4 against replacement_html in this file; cross-checked v22 quote attribution against raw-text/06-evolution.txt.

holes_reverified.evidence: Current prose frames the quote as "a related geometric failure in the past — but a smaller one" and scopes it to "cross-equatorial city-pair distances computed under V9/V11's circular-disc + WGS84 coordinate formula." The paragraph then distinguishes the 2× coordinate-formula problem from the 8–9× rim problem explicitly. A dome defender cannot fairly claim misrepresentation.

Current prose states the mapping inline: "Under the standard flat-earth azimuthal-equidistant mapping, where latitude φ projects linearly to disc radius via r = 20,015 × (90° − φ)/180°, the 70°S coast sits at r = 20,015 × 160/180 ≈ 17,791 km." The paragraph then pre-empts the "wrong projection" defense by noting the result is projection-independent: "any monotonic latitude→radius mapping that places Antarctica on or near the rim of a 20,015 km disc still yields a rim circumference of ~125,760 km... The disc radius, not the projection, sets the scale."

minor_precision_issue.description: The phrase "V9/V11's circular-disc + WGS84 coordinate formula" references V11 specifically, but V11 does not appear anywhere in the dome raw-text. The evolution log documents V9 (superseded by V12) and the v-prefix evolution series (v0.4, v31.0, v45.0, v48.0, v50.4, v50.5) but no V11. The retrospective note in raw-text/06-evolution.txt says only "This was correct for circular disc + WGS84 coordinates" without a specific version label.
minor_precision_issue.suggested_patch: find-and-replace: "V9/V11's circular-disc" → "V9-era circular-disc" (or "the pre-V12 circular-disc"). Either phrasing is supported by raw-text/06-evolution.txt and raw-text/04-coordinates.txt (which explicitly labels V9 as "Superseded by V12").
minor_precision_issue.impact_if_unpatched: A dome defender with a careful reading of the evolution log could point out that V11 is undocumented. This would not invalidate the argument — the point is that the circular-disc + WGS84 era preceded the current V12/V13 system, which is factually correct. But it is a small credibility dent. Low priority, suitable for a pass through a future prose-polish sweep.
minor_precision_issue.decider_action: Optional find-and-replace. Not worth its own issue unless the decider is already patching Section 7.1 for another reason.

verification_report.final_verdict: EXP-161 is substantively complete. Both moderate holes flagged in tracker notes are addressed in the integrated prose. One trivial precision issue (V11 attribution) remains and is logged for optional future cleanup.
